samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier with two levels of registers.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Multiplicand input signal.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Multiplier input signal.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="size" value="4">Size parameter for the multiplier.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign id="mul_a_ext" width_description="[7:0]">Extended multiplicand signal.</assign>
        <assign id="mul_b_ext" width_description="[7:0]">Extended multiplier signal.</assign>
        <assign id="partial_products" width_description="[7:0][3:0]">Array of partial products.</assign>
        <assign id="sum_reg" width_description="[7:0][3:0]">Register array for storing sums.</assign>
        <assign id="product" width_description="[7:0]">Final product signal.</assign>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Extend inputs by adding 'size' number of zeros at MSB positions
        assign mul_a_ext = {4'b0000, mul_a};
        assign mul_b_ext = {4'b0000, mul_b};
        
        // Generate partial products based on multiplier bits
        genvar i;
        generate
            for(i = 0; i < size; i = i + 1) begin : gen_partial_products
                assign partial_products[i] = (mul_b_ext[i]) ? (mul_a_ext << i) : 'd0;
            end
        endgenerate
        
        // Add partial products using registers
        integer j;
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                for(j = 0; j < size; j = j + 1) begin
                    sum_reg[j] <= 0;
                end
            end else begin
                for(j = 0; j < size; j = j + 1) begin
                    sum_reg[j] <= partial_products[j];
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Calculate final product using registers
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if(!rst_n) begin
                product <= 0;
            end else begin
                product <= sum_reg[0] + sum_reg[1] + sum_reg[2] + sum_reg[3];
            end
        end
        
        // Assign final product to output port
        assign mul_out = product;
    </implementation>
</module>
